questions.question: What was the main source of income for the British in India?
Who introduced the Permanent Settlement?
In which year was the Permanent Settlement introduced?
Who were zamindars?
questions.answer: Landowners who collected land revenue from peasants.

questions.question: Who cultivated the land in villages?
What is indigo?
questions.answer: A plant used to make blue dye.

questions.question: Why did the British encourage indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: To produce blue dye for cloth.

questions.question: Who forced peasants to grow indigo?
What was the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: A protest by peasants against indigo cultivation.

questions.question: When did the Indigo Revolt take place?
Which region was famous for indigo cultivation?
What type of crop is indigo?
What did planters give peasants to make them grow indigo?
Why did peasants dislike growing indigo?
questions.answer: It reduced land for food crops.

questions.question: Who provided loans to peasants in villages?
Why did peasants borrow money from moneylenders?
What happened to zamindars who failed to pay revenue?
What did peasants do during the Indigo Revolt?
Which commission was set up after the Indigo Revolt?
What problem did peasants face due to heavy taxes?
Who controlled indigo factories?
Why did peasants protest against planters?
questions.answer: They were forced to grow indigo.

questions.question: What happened to many peasants because of loans?
Why was indigo cultivation harmful to farmers?
questions.answer: It reduced soil fertility and food production.

questions.question: What does the Indigo Revolt show?
